NKJV.1: Do not rebuke an older man, but exhort him as a father, younger men as brothers,
NKJV.2: older women as mothers, younger women as sisters, with all purity.
NKJV.3: Honor widows who are really widows.
NKJV.4: But if any widow has children or grandchildren, let them first learn to show piety at home and to repay their parents; for this is good and acceptable before God.
NKJV.5: Now she who is really a widow, and left alone, trusts in God and continues in supplications and prayers night and day.
NKJV.6: But she who lives in pleasure is dead while she lives.
NKJV.7: And these things command, that they may be blameless.
NKJV.8: But if anyone does not provide for his own, and especially for those of his household, he has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
NKJV.9: Do not let a widow under sixty years old be taken into the number, and not unless she has been the wife of one man,
NKJV.10: well reported for good works: if she has brought up children, if she has lodged strangers, if she has washed the saints’ feet, if she has relieved the afflicted, if she has diligently followed every good work.
NKJV.11: But refuse the younger widows; for when they have begun to grow wanton against Christ, they desire to marry,
NKJV.12: having condemnation because they have cast off their first faith.
NKJV.13: And besides they learn to be idle, wandering about from house to house, and not only idle but also gossips and busybodies, saying things which they ought not.
NKJV.14: Therefore I desire that the younger widows marry, bear children, manage the house, give no opportunity to the adversary to speak reproachfully.
NKJV.15: For some have already turned aside after Satan.
NKJV.16: If any believing man or woman has widows, let them relieve them, and do not let the church be burdened, that it may relieve those who are really widows.
NKJV.17: Let the elders who rule well be counted worthy of double honor, especially those who labor in the word and doctrine.
NKJV.18: For the Scripture says, “You shall not muzzle an ox while it treads out the grain,” and, “The laborer is worthy of his wages.”
NKJV.19: Do not receive an accusation against an elder except from two or three witnesses.
NKJV.20: Those who are sinning rebuke in the presence of all, that the rest also may fear.
NKJV.21: I charge you before God and the Lord Jesus Christ and the elect angels that you observe these things without prejudice, doing nothing with partiality.
NKJV.22: Do not lay hands on anyone hastily, nor share in other people’s sins; keep yourself pure.
NKJV.23: No longer drink only water, but use a little wine for your stomach’s sake and your frequent infirmities.
NKJV.24: Some men’s sins are clearly evident, preceding them to judgment, but those of some men follow later.
NKJV.25: Likewise, the good works of some are clearly evident, and those that are otherwise cannot be hidden.

ESV.1: Do not rebuke an older man but encourage him as you would a father, younger men as brothers,
ESV.2: older women as mothers, younger women as sisters, in all purity.
ESV.3: Honor widows who are truly widows.
ESV.4: But if a widow has children or grandchildren, let them first learn to show godliness to their own household and to make some return to their parents, for this is pleasing in the sight of God.
ESV.5: She who is truly a widow, left all alone, has set her hope on God and continues in supplications and prayers night and day,
ESV.6: but she who is self-indulgent is dead even while she lives.
ESV.7: Command these things as well, so that they may be without reproach.
ESV.8: But if anyone does not provide for his relatives, and especially for members of his household, he has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
ESV.9: Let a widow be enrolled if she is not less than sixty years of age, having been the wife of one husband,
ESV.10: and having a reputation for good works: if she has brought up children, has shown hospitality, has washed the feet of the saints, has cared for the afflicted, and has devoted herself to every good work.
ESV.11: But refuse to enroll younger widows, for when their passions draw them away from Christ, they desire to marry
ESV.12: and so incur condemnation for having abandoned their former faith.
ESV.13: Besides that, they learn to be idlers, going about from house to house, and not only idlers, but also gossips and busybodies, saying what they should not.
ESV.14: So I would have younger widows marry, bear children, manage their households, and give the adversary no occasion for slander.
ESV.15: For some have already strayed after Satan.
ESV.16: If any believing woman has relatives who are widows, let her care for them. Let the church not be burdened, so that it may care for those who are truly widows.
ESV.17: Let the elders who rule well be considered worthy of double honor, especially those who labor in preaching and teaching.
ESV.18: For the Scripture says, “You shall not muzzle an ox when it treads out the grain,” and,  “The laborer deserves his wages.”
ESV.19: Do not admit a charge against an elder except on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
ESV.20: As for those who persist in sin, rebuke them in the presence of all, so that the rest may stand in fear.
ESV.21: In the presence of God and of Christ Jesus and of the elect angels I charge you to keep these rules without prejudging, doing nothing from partiality.
ESV.22: Do not be hasty in the laying on of hands, nor take part in the sins of others; keep yourself pure.
ESV.23: (No longer drink only water, but use a little wine for the sake of your stomach and your frequent ailments.)
ESV.24: The sins of some people are conspicuous, going before them to judgment, but the sins of others appear later.
ESV.25: So also good works are conspicuous, and even those that are not cannot remain hidden.

NASB.1: Do not sharply rebuke an older man, but [rather] appeal to [him] as a father, [to] the younger men as brothers,
NASB.2: the older women as mothers, [and] the younger women as sisters, in all purity.
NASB.3: Honor widows who are widows indeed;
NASB.4: but if any widow has children or grandchildren, they must first learn to practice piety in regard to their own family and to make some return to their parents; for this is acceptable in the sight of God.
NASB.5: Now she who is a widow indeed and who has been left alone, has fixed her hope on God and continues in entreaties and prayers night and day.
NASB.6: But she who gives herself to wanton pleasure is dead even while she lives.
NASB.7: Prescribe these things as well, so that they may be above reproach.
NASB.8: But if anyone does not provide for his own, and especially for those of his household, he has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
NASB.9: A widow is to be put on the list only if she is not less than sixty years old, [having been] the wife of one man,
NASB.10: having a reputation for good works; [and] if she has brought up children, if she has shown hospitality to strangers, if she has washed the saints' feet, if she has assisted those in distress, [and] if she has devoted herself to every good work.
NASB.11: But refuse [to put] younger widows [on the list], for when they feel sensual desires in disregard of Christ, they want to get married,
NASB.12: [thus] incurring condemnation, because they have set aside their previous pledge.
NASB.13: At the same time they also learn [to be] idle, as they go around from house to house; and not merely idle, but also gossips and busybodies, talking about things not proper [to mention].
NASB.14: Therefore, I want younger [widows] to get married, bear children, keep house, [and] give the enemy no occasion for reproach;
NASB.15: for some have already turned aside to follow Satan.
NASB.16: If any woman who is a believer has [dependent] widows, she must assist them and the church must not be burdened, so that it may assist those who are widows indeed.
NASB.17: The elders who rule well are to be considered worthy of double honor, especially those who work hard at preaching and teaching.
NASB.18: For the Scripture says, 'YOU SHALL NOT MUZZLE THE OX WHILE HE IS THRESHING,' and 'The laborer is worthy of his wages.'
NASB.19: Do not receive an accusation against an elder except on the basis of two or three witnesses.
NASB.20: Those who continue in sin, rebuke in the presence of all, so that the rest also will be fearful [of sinning].
NASB.21: I solemnly charge you in the presence of God and of Christ Jesus and of [His] chosen angels, to maintain these [principles] without bias, doing nothing in a [spirit of] partiality.
NASB.22: Do not lay hands upon anyone [too] hastily and thereby share [responsibility for] the sins of others; keep yourself free from sin.
NASB.23: No longer drink water [exclusively], but use a little wine for the sake of your stomach and your frequent ailments.
NASB.24: The sins of some men are quite evident, going before them to judgment; for others, their [sins] follow after.
NASB.25: Likewise also, deeds that are good are quite evident, and those which are otherwise cannot be concealed.

NLT.1: Never speak harshly to an older man, but appeal to him respectfully as you would to your own father. Talk to younger men as you would to your own brothers.
NLT.2: Treat older women as you would your mother, and treat younger women with all purity as you would your own sisters.
NLT.3: Take care of any widow who has no one else to care for her.
NLT.4: But if she has children or grandchildren, their first responsibility is to show godliness at home and repay their parents by taking care of them. This is something that pleases God.
NLT.5: Now a true widow, a woman who is truly alone in this world, has placed her hope in God. She prays night and day, asking God for his help.
NLT.6: But the widow who lives only for pleasure is spiritually dead even while she lives.
NLT.7: Give these instructions to the church so that no one will be open to criticism.
NLT.8: But those who won’t care for their relatives, especially those in their own household, have denied the true faith. Such people are worse than unbelievers.
NLT.9: A widow who is put on the list for support must be a woman who is at least sixty years old and was faithful to her husband.
NLT.10: She must be well respected by everyone because of the good she has done. Has she brought up her children well? Has she been kind to strangers and served other believers humbly? Has she helped those who are in trouble? Has she always been ready to do good?
NLT.11: The younger widows should not be on the list, because their physical desires will overpower their devotion to Christ and they will want to remarry.
NLT.12: Then they would be guilty of breaking their previous pledge.
NLT.13: And if they are on the list, they will learn to be lazy and will spend their time gossiping from house to house, meddling in other people’s business and talking about things they shouldn’t.
NLT.14: So I advise these younger widows to marry again, have children, and take care of their own homes. Then the enemy will not be able to say anything against them.
NLT.15: For I am afraid that some of them have already gone astray and now follow Satan.
NLT.16: If a woman who is a believer has relatives who are widows, she must take care of them and not put the responsibility on the church. Then the church can care for the widows who are truly alone.
NLT.17: Elders who do their work well should be respected and paid well, especially those who work hard at both preaching and teaching.
NLT.18: For the Scripture says, “You must not muzzle an ox to keep it from eating as it treads out the grain.” And in another place, “Those who work deserve their pay!”
NLT.19: Do not listen to an accusation against an elder unless it is confirmed by two or three witnesses.
NLT.20: Those who sin should be reprimanded in front of the whole church; this will serve as a strong warning to others.
NLT.21: I solemnly command you in the presence of God and Christ Jesus and the highest angels to obey these instructions without taking sides or showing favoritism to anyone.
NLT.22: Never be in a hurry about appointing a church leader. Do not share in the sins of others. Keep yourself pure.
NLT.23: Don’t drink only water. You ought to drink a little wine for the sake of your stomach because you are sick so often.
NLT.24: Remember, the sins of some people are obvious, leading them to certain judgment. But there are others whose sins will not be revealed until later.
NLT.25: In the same way, the good deeds of some people are obvious. And the good deeds done in secret will someday come to light.

WEB.1: Don’t rebuke an older man, but exhort him as a father; the younger men as brothers;
WEB.2: the elder women as mothers; the younger as sisters, in all purity.
WEB.3: Honor widows who are widows indeed.
WEB.4: But if any widow has children or grandchildren, let them learn first to show piety towards their own family, and to repay their parents, for this is  acceptable in the sight of God.
WEB.5: Now she who is a widow indeed, and desolate, has her hope set on God, and continues in petitions and prayers night and day.
WEB.6: But she who gives herself to pleasure is dead while she lives.
WEB.7: Also command these things, that they may be without reproach.
WEB.8: But if anyone doesn’t provide for his own, and especially his own household, he has denied the faith, and is worse than an unbeliever.
WEB.9: Let no one be enrolled as a widow under sixty years old, having been the wife of one man,
WEB.10: being approved by good works, if she has brought up children, if she has been hospitable to strangers, if she has washed the saints’ feet, if she has relieved the afflicted, and if she has diligently followed every good work.
WEB.11: But refuse younger widows, for when they have grown wanton against Christ, they desire to marry;
WEB.12: having condemnation, because they have rejected their first pledge.
WEB.13: Besides, they also learn to be idle, going about from house to house. Not only idle, but also gossips and busybodies, saying things which they ought not.
WEB.14: I desire therefore that the younger widows marry, bear children, rule the household, and give no occasion to the adversary for insulting.
WEB.15: For already some have turned aside after Satan.
WEB.16: If any man or woman who believes has widows, let them relieve them, and don’t let the assembly be burdened; that it might relieve those who are widows indeed.
WEB.17: Let the elders who rule well be counted worthy of double honor, especially those who labor in the word and in teaching.
WEB.18: For the Scripture says, “You shall not muzzle the ox when it treads out the grain.”  And, “The laborer is worthy of his wages.”
WEB.19: Don’t receive an accusation against an elder, except at the word of two or three witnesses.
WEB.20: Those who sin, reprove in the sight of all, that the rest also may be in fear.
WEB.21: I command you in the sight of God, and Christ Jesus, and the chosen angels, that you observe these things without prejudice, doing nothing by partiality.
WEB.22: Lay hands hastily on no one, neither be a participant in other men’s sins. Keep yourself pure.
WEB.23: Be no longer a drinker of water only, but use a little wine for your stomach’s sake and your frequent infirmities.
WEB.24: Some men’s sins are evident, preceding them to judgment, and some also follow later.
WEB.25: In the same way also there are good works that are obvious, and those that are otherwise can’t be hidden.

CSB17.1: Don’t rebuke an older man, but exhort him as a father, younger men as brothers,
CSB17.2: older women as mothers, and the younger women as sisters with all purity.
CSB17.3: Support <sup>[1]</sup> widows <sup>ⓖ</sup> who are genuinely in need.
CSB17.4: But if any widow has children or grandchildren, let them learn to practice godliness toward their own family first and to repay their parents, for this pleases God.
CSB17.5: The widow who is truly in need and left all alone has put her hope in God <sup>ⓗ</sup> and continues night and day in her petitions and prayers;
CSB17.6: however, she who is self-indulgent is dead even while she lives. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.7: Command this also, so that they will be above reproach.
CSB17.8: But if anyone does not provide <sup>ⓙ</sup> for his own family, especially for his own household, he has denied the faith <sup>ⓚ</sup> and is worse than an unbeliever.
CSB17.9: No widow is to be enrolled on the list for support unless she is at least sixty years old, has been the wife of one husband,
CSB17.10: and is well known for good works <sup>ⓛ</sup>  — that is, if she has brought up children, shown hospitality, <sup>ⓜ</sup> washed the saints’ feet, <sup>ⓝ</sup> helped the afflicted, <sup>ⓐ</sup> and devoted herself to every good work. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.11: But refuse to enroll younger widows, for when they are drawn away from Christ by desire, they want to marry <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.12: and will therefore receive condemnation <sup>ⓓ</sup> because they have renounced their original pledge.
CSB17.13: At the same time, they also learn to be idle, going from house to house; they are not only idle, but are also gossips <sup>ⓔ</sup> and busybodies, saying things they shouldn’t say.
CSB17.14: Therefore, I want younger women to marry, <sup>ⓕ</sup> have children, manage their households, and give the adversary no opportunity to accuse us. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.15: For some have already turned away <sup>ⓗ</sup> to follow Satan.
CSB17.16: If any <sup>[2]</sup> believing woman has widows in her family, let her help them. Let the church not be burdened, so that it can help widows in genuine need.
CSB17.17: The elders who are good leaders are to be considered worthy of double honor, <sup>[3]</sup> especially those who work hard <sup>ⓘ</sup> at preaching and teaching.
CSB17.18: For the Scripture says: Do not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain, [4] ⓙ and the worker is worthy of his wages. ⓚ
CSB17.19: Don’t accept an accusation against an elder unless it is supported by two or three witnesses. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.20: Publicly rebuke those who sin, so that the rest will be afraid.
CSB17.21: I solemnly charge you before God and Christ Jesus and the elect angels to observe <sup>ⓜ</sup> these things without prejudice, doing nothing out of favoritism.
CSB17.22: Don’t be too quick to appoint <sup>[5]</sup> <sup>ⓝ</sup> anyone as an elder, and don’t share in the sins of others. Keep yourself pure. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.23: Don’t continue drinking only water, but use a little wine because of your stomach and your frequent illnesses.
CSB17.24: Some people’s sins are obvious, preceding them to judgment, <sup>ⓑ</sup> but the sins of others surface <sup>[6]</sup> later.
CSB17.25: Likewise, good works are obvious, and those that are not obvious cannot remain hidden.

AMP.1: Do not sharply reprimand an older man, but appeal to him as [you would to] a father, to younger men as brothers,
AMP.2: to older women as mothers, to younger women as sisters, in all purity [being careful to maintain appropriate relationships].
AMP.3: Honor and help those widows who are truly widowed [alone, and without support].
AMP.4: But if a widow has children or grandchildren [who are adults], see to it that these first learn to show great respect to their own family [as their religious duty and natural obligation], and to compensate their parents or grandparents [for their upbringing]; for this is acceptable and pleasing in the sight of God.
AMP.5: Now a woman who is really a widow and has been left [entirely] alone [without adequate income] trusts in God and continues in supplications and prayers night and day.
AMP.6: Whereas she who lives for pleasure and self-indulgence is spiritually dead even while she still lives.
AMP.7: Keep instructing [the people to do] these things as well, so that they may be blameless and beyond reproach.
AMP.8: If anyone fails to provide for his own, and especially for those of his own family, he has denied the faith [by disregarding its precepts] and is worse than an unbeliever [who fulfills his obligation in these matters].
AMP.9: A widow is to be put on the list [to receive regular assistance] only if she is over sixty years of age, [having been] the wife of one man,
AMP.10: and has a reputation for good deeds; [she is eligible] if she has brought up children, if she has shown hospitality to strangers, if she has washed the feet of the saints (God's people), if she has assisted the distressed, and has devoted herself to doing good in every way.
AMP.11: But refuse [to enroll the] younger widows, for when they feel their natural desires in disregard of Christ, they wish to marry again,
AMP.12: and so they incur condemnation for having set aside their previous pledge.
AMP.13: Now at the same time, they also learn to be idle as they go from house to house; and not only idle, but also gossips and busybodies [meddlers in things that do not concern them], talking about things they should not mention.
AMP.14: So I want younger widows to get married, have children, manage their households, and not give opponents of the faith any occasion for slander.
AMP.15: Some [widows] have already turned away [from the faith] to follow Satan.
AMP.16: If any believing woman has [dependent] widows [in her household], she must assist them [according to her ability]; and the church must not be burdened [with them], so that it may assist those who are truly widows [those who are all alone and are dependent].
AMP.17: The elders who perform their leadership duties well are to be considered worthy of double honor (financial support), especially those who work hard at preaching and teaching [the word of God concerning eternal salvation through Christ].
AMP.18: For the Scripture says, "You shall not muzzle the ox while it is treading out the grain [to keep it from eating]," and, "The worker is worthy of his wages [he deserves fair compensation]."
AMP.19: Do not accept an accusation against an elder unless it is based on [the testimony of at least] two or three witnesses.
AMP.20: As for those [elders] who continue in sin, reprimand them in the presence of all [the congregation], so that the rest will be warned.
AMP.21: I solemnly charge you in the presence of God and of Christ Jesus and of His chosen angels that you guard and keep these rules without bias, doing nothing out of favoritism.
AMP.22: Do not hurry to lay hands on anyone [ordaining and approving someone for ministry or an office in the church, or in reinstating expelled offenders], and thereby share in the sins of others; keep yourself free from sin.
AMP.23: No longer continue drinking [only] water, but use a little wine for the sake of your stomach and your frequent illnesses.
AMP.24: The sins of some people are conspicuous, leading the way for them into judgment [so that they are clearly not qualified for ministry]; but the sins of others appear later [for they are hidden and follow behind them].
AMP.25: Likewise, good deeds are quite evident, and those which are otherwise cannot be hidden [indefinitely].

MSG.1: Don't be harsh or impatient with an older man. Talk to him as you would your own father, and to the younger men as your brothers.
MSG.2: Reverently honor an older woman as you would your mother, and the younger women as sisters.
MSG.3: Take care of widows who are destitute.
MSG.4: If a widow has family members to take care of her, let them learn that religion begins at their own doorstep and that they should pay back with gratitude some of what they have received. This pleases God immensely.
MSG.5: You can tell a legitimate widow by the way she has put all her hope in God, praying to him constantly for the needs of others as well as her own.
MSG.6: But a widow who exploits people's emotions and pocketbooks—well, there's nothing to her.
MSG.7: Tell these things to the people so that they will do the right thing in their extended family.
MSG.8: Anyone who neglects to care for family members in need repudiates the faith. That's worse than refusing to believe in the first place.
MSG.9: Sign some widows up for the special ministry of offering assistance. They will in turn receive support from the church. They must be over sixty, married only once,
MSG.10: and have a reputation for helping out with children, strangers, tired Christians, the hurt and troubled.
MSG.11: Don't put young widows on this list. No sooner will they get on than they'll want to get off, obsessed with wanting to get a husband rather than serving Christ in this way.
MSG.12: By breaking their word, they're liable to go from bad to worse,
MSG.13: frittering away their days on empty talk, gossip, and trivialities.
MSG.14: No, I'd rather the young widows go ahead and get married in the first place, have children, manage their homes, and not give critics any foothold for finding fault.
MSG.15: Some of them have already left and gone after Satan.
MSG.16: Any Christian woman who has widows in her family is responsible for them. They shouldn't be dumped on the church. The church has its hands full already with widows who need help.
MSG.17: Give a bonus to leaders who do a good job, especially the ones who work hard at preaching and teaching.
MSG.18: Scripture tells us, "Don't muzzle a working ox," and, "A worker deserves his pay."
MSG.19: Don't listen to a complaint against a leader that isn't backed up by two or three responsible witnesses.
MSG.20: If anyone falls into sin, call that person on the carpet. Those who are inclined that way will know right off they can't get by with it.
MSG.21: God and Jesus and angels all back me up in these instructions. Carry them out without favoritism, without taking sides.
MSG.22: Don't appoint people to church leadership positions too hastily. If a person is involved in some serious sins, you don't want to become an unwitting accomplice. In any event, keep a close check on yourself.
MSG.23: And don't worry too much about what the critics will say. Go ahead and drink a little wine, for instance; it's good for your digestion, good medicine for what ails you.
MSG.24: The sins of some people are blatant and march them right into court. The sins of others don't show up until much later.
MSG.25: The same with good deeds. Some you see right off, but none are hidden forever.

NIV.1:
Advice About Widows, Elders and Slaves
Do not rebuke an older man harshly, but exhort him as if he were your father. Treat younger men as brothers,
NIV.2: older women as mothers, and younger women as sisters, with absolute purity.
NIV.3: Give proper recognition to those widows who are really in need.
NIV.4: But if a widow has children or grandchildren, these should learn first of all to put their religion into practice by caring for their own family and so repaying their parents and grandparents, for this is pleasing to God.
NIV.5: The widow who is really in need and left all alone puts her hope in God and continues night and day to pray and to ask God for help.
NIV.6: But the widow who lives for pleasure is dead even while she lives.
NIV.7: Give the people these instructions, too, so that no one may be open to blame.
NIV.8: If anyone does not provide for his relatives, and especially for his immediate family, he has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
NIV.9: No widow may be put on the list of widows unless she is over sixty, has been faithful to her husband,
NIV.10: and is well known for her good deeds, such as bringing up children, showing hospitality, washing the feet of the saints, helping those in trouble and devoting herself to all kinds of good deeds.
NIV.11: As for younger widows, do not put them on such a list. For when their sensual desires overcome their dedication to Christ, they want to marry.
NIV.12: Thus they bring judgment on themselves, because they have broken their first pledge.
NIV.13: Besides, they get into the habit of being idle and going about from house to house. And not only do they become idlers, but also gossips and busybodies, saying things they ought not to.
NIV.14: So I counsel younger widows to marry, to have children, to manage their homes and to give the enemy no opportunity for slander.
NIV.15: Some have in fact already turned away to follow Satan.
NIV.16: If any woman who is a believer has widows in her family, she should help them and not let the church be burdened with them, so that the church can help those widows who are really in need.
NIV.17: The elders who direct the affairs of the church well are worthy of double honor, especially those whose work is preaching and teaching.
NIV.18: For the Scripture says, “Do not muzzle the ox while it is treading out the grain,” and “The worker deserves his wages.”
NIV.19: Do not entertain an accusation against an elder unless it is brought by two or three witnesses.
NIV.20: Those who sin are to be rebuked publicly, so that the others may take warning.
NIV.21: I charge you, in the sight of God and Christ Jesus and the elect angels, to keep these instructions without partiality, and to do nothing out of favoritism.
NIV.22: Do not be hasty in the laying on of hands, and do not share in the sins of others. Keep yourself pure.
NIV.23: Stop drinking only water, and use a little wine because of your stomach and your frequent illnesses.
NIV.24: The sins of some men are obvious, reaching the place of judgment ahead of them; the sins of others trail behind them.
NIV.25: In the same way, good deeds are obvious, and even those that are not cannot be hidden.

NRSVCE.1: Do not speak harshly to an older man, but speak to him as to a father, to younger men as brothers,
NRSVCE.2: to older women as mothers, to younger women as sisters — with absolute purity.
NRSVCE.3: Honor widows who are really widows.
NRSVCE.4: If a widow has children or grandchildren, they should first learn their religious duty to their own family and make some repayment to their parents; for this is pleasing in God’s sight.
NRSVCE.5: The real widow, left alone, has set her hope on God and continues in supplications and prayers night and day;
NRSVCE.6: but the widow who lives for pleasure is dead even while she lives.
NRSVCE.7: Give these commands as well, so that they may be above reproach.
NRSVCE.8: And whoever does not provide for relatives, and especially for family members, has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
NRSVCE.9: Let a widow be put on the list if she is not less than sixty years old and has been married only once;
NRSVCE.10: she must be well attested for her good works, as one who has brought up children, shown hospitality, washed the saints’ feet, helped the afflicted, and devoted herself to doing good in every way.
NRSVCE.11: But refuse to put younger widows on the list; for when their sensual desires alienate them from Christ, they want to marry,
NRSVCE.12: and so they incur condemnation for having violated their first pledge.
NRSVCE.13: Besides that, they learn to be idle, gadding about from house to house; and they are not merely idle, but also gossips and busybodies, saying what they should not say.
NRSVCE.14: So I would have younger widows marry, bear children, and manage their households, so as to give the adversary no occasion to revile us.
NRSVCE.15: For some have already turned away to follow Satan.
NRSVCE.16: If any believing woman has relatives who are really widows, let her assist them; let the church not be burdened, so that it can assist those who are real widows.
NRSVCE.17: Let the elders who rule well be considered worthy of double honor, especially those who labor in preaching and teaching;
NRSVCE.18: for the scripture says, “You shall not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain,” and, “The laborer deserves to be paid.”
NRSVCE.19: Never accept any accusation against an elder except on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
NRSVCE.20: As for those who persist in sin, rebuke them in the presence of all, so that the rest also may stand in fear.
NRSVCE.21: In the presence of God and of Christ Jesus and of the elect angels, I warn you to keep these instructions without prejudice, doing nothing on the basis of partiality.
NRSVCE.22: Do not ordain anyone hastily, and do not participate in the sins of others; keep yourself pure.
NRSVCE.23: No longer drink only water, but take a little wine for the sake of your stomach and your frequent ailments.
NRSVCE.24: The sins of some people are conspicuous and precede them to judgment, while the sins of others follow them there.
NRSVCE.25: So also good works are conspicuous; and even when they are not, they cannot remain hidden.

NET.1: Do not address an older man harshly but appeal to him as a father. Speak to younger men as brothers,
NET.2: older women as mothers, and younger women as sisters — with complete purity.
NET.3: Honor widows who are truly in need.
NET.4: But if a widow has children or grandchildren, they should first learn to fulfill their duty toward their own household and so repay their parents what is owed them. For this is what pleases God.
NET.5: But the widow who is truly in need, and completely on her own, has set her hope on God and continues in her pleas and prayers night and day.
NET.6: But the one who lives for pleasure is dead even while she lives.
NET.7: Reinforce these commands, so that they will be beyond reproach.
NET.8: But if someone does not provide for his own, especially his own family, he has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
NET.9: No widow should be put on the list unless she is at least sixty years old, was the wife of one husband,
NET.10: and has a reputation for good works: as one who has raised children, practiced hospitality, washed the feet of the saints, helped those in distress — as one who has exhibited all kinds of good works.
NET.11: But do not accept younger widows on the list, because their passions may lead them away from Christ and they will desire to marry,
NET.12: and so incur judgment for breaking their former pledge.
NET.13: And besides that, going around from house to house they learn to be lazy, and they are not only lazy, but also gossips and busybodies, talking about things they should not.
NET.14: So I want younger women to marry, raise children, and manage a household, in order to give the adversary no opportunity to vilify us.
NET.15: For some have already wandered away to follow Satan.
NET.16: If a believing woman has widows in her family, let her help them. The church should not be burdened, so that it may help the widows who are truly in need.
NET.17: Elders who provide effective leadership must be counted worthy of double honor, especially those who work hard in speaking and teaching.
NET.18: For the scripture says, “Do not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain,” and, “The worker deserves his pay.”
NET.19: Do not accept an accusation against an elder unless it can be confirmed by two or three witnesses.
NET.20: Those guilty of sin must be rebuked before all, as a warning to the rest.
NET.21: Before God and Christ Jesus and the elect angels, I solemnly charge you to carry out these commands without prejudice or favoritism of any kind.
NET.22: Do not lay hands on anyone hastily and so identify with the sins of others. Keep yourself pure.
NET.23: (Stop drinking just water, but use a little wine for your digestion and your frequent illnesses.)
NET.24: The sins of some people are obvious, going before them into judgment, but for others, they show up later.
NET.25: Similarly good works are also obvious, and the ones that are not cannot remain hidden.
